diagrams.flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[User Visits Website] --> B{Has Account?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Login]
    B -->|No| D[Click Sign Up]
    C --> E{MFA Enabled?}
    E -->|Yes| F[Enter 2FA Code]
    E -->|No| G[Dashboard]
    F --> G
    D --> H[Enter Email & Password]
    H --> I[Verify Email Link]
    I --> J{Email Verified?}
    J -->|No| K[Resend Email]
    K --> I
    J -->|Yes| L[Choose Plan]
    L --> M{Free or Paid?}
    M -->|Free| N[Onboarding]
    M -->|Paid| O[Enter Payment Info]
    O --> P[Process Payment]
    P --> Q{Payment Success?}
    Q -->|No| R[Error Page]
    R --> O
    Q -->|Yes| N
    N --> G
diagrams.state:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Free
    Free --> Trial: Start Trial
    Trial --> Active: Start Paid
    Free --> Cancelled: Cancel
    Active --> Active: Renew Subscription
    Active --> Paused: Pause Billing
    Paused --> Active: Resume
    Paused --> Cancelled: Cancel While Paused
    Active --> Cancelled: Cancel
    Active --> Overdue: Payment Failed
    Overdue --> Active: Payment Recovered
    Overdue --> Suspended: Days Overdue > 7
    Suspended --> Active: Manual Payment Received
    Suspended --> Cancelled: Auto-cancel
    Trial --> Cancelled: Trial Expired
    Cancelled --> [*]
diagrams.sequence:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Meridian as Meridian App
    participant Payment as Payment Gateway
    participant Stripe as Stripe API
    participant Database as Meridian DB
    User->>Meridian: Click Upgrade Plan
    Meridian->>Payment: Validate Plan
    Payment-->>Meridian: Plan Valid
    Meridian->>User: Show Payment Form
    User->>Meridian: Enter Card Details
    Meridian->>Payment: Encrypt & Store Card
    Payment-->>Meridian: Card Token
    Meridian->>Stripe: Create Charge
    Stripe->>Stripe: Process Payment
    Stripe-->>Meridian: Payment Success / Failure
    alt Payment Success
        Meridian->>Database: Update Subscription
        Database-->>Meridian: Updated
        Meridian->>Meridian: Send Confirmation Email
        Meridian-->>User: Success Page
    else Payment Failed
        Meridian-->>User: Error Message
        User->>Meridian: Retry Payment
    end
diagrams.timeline:
timeline
    title Meridian 2026 Roadmap
    section Q1
        Jan : MVP Launch : Email Auth
        Feb : Payment Integration : Stripe Webhook
        Mar : Billing Reporting : Monthly Metrics
    section Q2
        Apr : Enterprise SSO : SAML Support
        May : API v1 Release : REST Endpoints
        Jun : Advanced Analytics : Cohort Analysis
    section Q3
        Jul : Mobile App : iOS/Android Beta
        Aug : Custom Reports : Report Builder
        Sep : Team Collaboration : Workspace Sharing
    section Q4
        Oct : Data Export : CSV/JSON
        Nov : Integrations : Zapier/Segment
        Dec : Performance Optimization : Query Caching
diagrams.class:
classDiagram
    class User {
        -id: UUID
        -email: string
        -password_hash: string
        -created_at: datetime
        +login()
        +logout()
        +updateProfile()
    }
    class Subscription {
        -id: UUID
        -user_id: UUID
        -plan_id: UUID
        -status: enum
        -started_at: datetime
        -ends_at: datetime
        +upgrade()
        +downgrade()
        +cancel()
    }
    class Plan {
        -id: UUID
        -name: string
        -price: float
        -features: array
        -max_events: integer
    }
    class Invoice {
        -id: UUID
        -subscription_id: UUID
        -amount: float
        -date: datetime
        -status: enum
        +sendEmail()
        +markPaid()
    }
    class Analytics {
        -id: UUID
        -subscription_id: UUID
        -events_count: integer
        -mrr: float
        -churn_risk: boolean
    }
    User --> Subscription
    Subscription --> Plan
    Subscription --> Invoice
    Subscription --> Analytics
diagrams.er:
erDiagram
    USERS ||--o{ SUBSCRIPTIONS : has
    USERS ||--o{ INVOICES : receives
    SUBSCRIPTIONS ||--|| PLANS : uses
    SUBSCRIPTIONS ||--o{ ANALYTICS : generates
    SUBSCRIPTIONS ||--o{ EVENTS : tracks
    INVOICES ||--|| PLANS : references
    ANALYTICS ||--|| SUBSCRIPTIONS : measures
    
    USERS {
        uuid id PK
        string email UK
        string password_hash
        datetime created_at
        datetime updated_at
    }
    SUBSCRIPTIONS {
        uuid id PK
        uuid user_id FK
        uuid plan_id FK
        enum status
        datetime started_at
        datetime ends_at
    }
    PLANS {
        uuid id PK
        string name UK
        float price
        integer max_events
        text features
    }
    INVOICES {
        uuid id PK
        uuid subscription_id FK
        float amount
        datetime issue_date
        enum status
    }
    ANALYTICS {
        uuid id PK
        uuid subscription_id FK
        integer events_this_month
        float mrr
        float churn_score
    }
    EVENTS {
        uuid id PK
        uuid subscription_id FK
        string event_type
        datetime timestamp
        text properties
    }
diagrams.journey:
journey
    title Customer Journey in Meridian
    section Awareness
        Discover Product: 5: User
        Read Pricing Page: 5: User
        Sign Up Free Trial: 5: User
    section Activation
        Complete Onboarding: 5: User, Product
        Connect Data Source: 4: User, Product
        View First Dashboard: 4: User, Product
    section Engagement
        Explore Analytics: 4: User, Product
        Create Custom Report: 5: User, Product
        Invite Team Member: 5: User, Product
    section Monetization
        Start Paid Plan: 4: User, Product, Billing
        Set Up Auto Renewal: 5: User, Billing
        Add Payment Method: 4: User, Billing
    section Retention
        View Monthly Dashboard: 4: User, Product
        Receive Weekly Email: 5: Product
        Attend Webinar: 3: Product
diagrams.architecture:
architecture-beta
    Frontend["Web App"]

    Gateway["API Gateway"]
    Auth["Auth Service"]
    Core["Analytics Engine"]
    Billing["Billing Service"]
    Cache["Redis Cache"]
    DB["PostgreSQL"]
    Queue["RabbitMQ"]
    Stripe["Stripe API"]
    
    Frontend --> Gateway
    Mobile --> Gateway
    Gateway --> Auth
    Gateway --> Core
    Gateway --> Billing
    Core --> Cache
    Core --> DB
    Auth --> DB
    Billing --> Queue
    Billing --> Stripe
    Queue --> DB

discovery: Ran 'am --help': Discovered all main CLI verbs including render, verify, parse, capabilities
Ran 'am capabilities': Discovered all 12 diagram families (flowchart, state, sequence, timeline, class, er, journey, architecture, xychart, pie, quadrant, gantt) with mutation ops and warning codes
Created flowchart diagram for Meridian signup flow: Verified clean with 'am verify' (no warnings)
Created state diagram for subscription billing lifecycle: Verified clean
Created sequence diagram for payment processing flow: Verified clean
Created timeline diagram for 2026 roadmap: Verified clean
Created class diagram for data model: Verified clean
Created ER diagram for database schema with 6 entities: Verified clean
Created journey diagram for customer lifecycle: Verified clean
Discovered architecture-beta header (not plain 'architecture') via trial and error with parse command
Created architecture diagram for system design: Verified clean (rendered as opaque but valid)
Created xychart-beta diagram for KPI metrics: Verified clean
Created pie chart for plan distribution: Verified clean
Created quadrant chart (initially failed due to [x,y] bracket syntax requirement): Fixed and verified clean
Created gantt chart for Q2 roadmap: Verified clean
Ran 'am llms-txt': Discovered all families support parse, verify, render, serialize, mutate
gaps: Architecture family uses 'architecture-beta' header; unclear if this is stable API or beta-only syntax
Some diagram families (architecture) are rendered as opaque/preserved source rather than fully structured AST—limits mutation capability
No discoverable examples or syntax guide in CLI help; discovered via parse error messages and trial-and-error
Quadrant point format [x,y] not immediately obvious from error message (required learning cycle)
mechanisms: am --help: Lists all verbs and flags; entry point for capability discovery
am capabilities: Returns JSON with family list, mutation ops per family, warning codes tier/severity
am verify --json: Structured output with ok/warnings/layout; errors show codes like EMPTY_DIAGRAM, UNSUPPORTED_SYNTAX
am parse --json: Returns parsed diagram structure or error with UNKNOWN_HEADER/PARSE_FAILED codes
am llms-txt: Comprehensive plain-text reference showing all families, verbs, output formats, mutation policy
Error messages during parse (PARSE_FAILED, UNKNOWN_HEADER) guide syntax discovery
Exit code 3 indicates verify-reported errors; 0 indicates success; enables automated validation loops
